questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A block with an initial speed of 10 m/s slides on a horizontal surface and comes to rest after travelling a distance of 25 m. What is the coefficient of kinetic friction? (g = 9.8 m/s²)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: If the length of a simple pendulum is 120 cm, calculate its frequency. [π = 22/7, g = 10 ms⁻²]

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: When a spiral spring is compressed by 200 N, it stores 0.16 J of energy. What energy will it store when compressed by 700 N?

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A stone is thrown vertically upward with a velocity of 20 m/s. How high does it go? (g = 10 m/s²)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is a scalar quantity?
questions.explanation: Speed has magnitude only (no direction) — it is a scalar. The rest are vectors.

questions.subject: Computer Science
questions.question: The first calculator was built by?
questions.explanation: Blaise Pascal invented the Pascaline mechanical calculator in 1642.

questions.subject: Computer Science
questions.question: What does CPU stand for?
questions.explanation: CPU = Central Processing Unit — it fetches and executes program instructions.

questions.subject: Computer Science
questions.question: What is the binary equivalent of the decimal number 13?

questions.subject: Computer Science
questions.question: Which generation of computers used transistors?
questions.explanation: Second generation computers (1956–1963) replaced vacuum tubes with transistors.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Find the value of x if 3x − 7 = 14

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: What is the area of a circle with radius 7 cm? (π = 22/7)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: What is the chemical symbol for Gold?
questions.explanation: Au comes from Gold's Latin name 'Aurum'.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: What is the pH of a neutral solution at 25°C?
questions.explanation: A neutral solution has exactly pH 7 at 25°C.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: What is the powerhouse of the cell?
questions.explanation: Mitochondria produce ATP through cellular respiration — hence the 'powerhouse' nickname.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which blood group is the universal donor?
questions.explanation: O negative lacks A, B, and Rh antigens — it can donate to anyone.

questions.subject: English
questions.question: Choose the word closest in meaning to "OBSTINATE"
questions.explanation: Obstinate = stubbornly refusing to change one's opinion or course of action.

questions.subject: English
questions.question: "The wind whispered through the trees" — identify the figure of speech.
questions.explanation: Personification — giving human traits (whispering) to a non-human subject (wind).
